channel.title: Seeking my first critique!
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Seeking my first critique!"

item.title: Re: Seeking my first critique!
item.content:encoded: That's much better. The only problem I have, if you can call it that, is that 'Agent Vega' is a pretty cheezy name for someone who is supposed to be a more realistic equivalent of a secret agent for a very serious foundation. Code names are fine, but something a little less on the nose.

item.title: Re: Seeking my first critique!
item.content:encoded: gotcha! I've been working on the idea to make it a bit more interesting and current, so I hope you'll like the changes I've made.

item.title: Re: Seeking my first critique!
item.content:encoded: It's not unique enough. Maybe loose the, "sad to be alive," part. I don't mind giving a sentient anomaly a personality but this is fairly common and uninteresting. "A small cube of flesh," isn't very descriptive. Go into more detail. Is it a perfectly smooth cube or does it have pertrusions? Is it just a hunk of flesh that produces a liquid? This idea suffers from a lack of originality.You should make the discovery more interesting. Instead of being found in a hospital scheduled for demolition, why not being found by police in the basement of a cannibal? Maybe it was created from the remains of one of his former victims? I'm not saying thats exactly what it should be, but think of something more unique, something that will surprise readers.

item.title: Seeking my first critique!
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article
Genre : Emotional

Elevator Pitch: A small cube of human flesh was 'born' in an abandoned maternity ward. It exudes emotional control over those near it, affecting one Foundation agent quite radically.

Central Narrative: Agent Vega, an operative on the scene during initial containment, becomes obsessed with taking care of the cube, and perceives it as a human baby, and as his own biological son.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The cube feeds on being cared for like a baby, and is not necessarily malicious on its own. The cube isn't sentient and the actions of weeping and crying like a child are just biological impulses it has to survive. Ultimately, the SCP has no feelings and just wants to live, on the very base level that simple mammals do.

Additional Notes: I don't have anything of particular worry but I am excited for folks to take a look! I have my sandbox ready to go whenever someone wants to take a look.
